spec_match.rationale: Six scenarios invoke a real same-origin handler and observe the exact `DOMException.name` returned by `PaymentRequest.show()` — matches the spec's AbortError-vs-OperationError contract.

interactive.rationale: Scenario buttons + install/canMakePayment controls + live counters + step-by-step promise trace render after each run; no static-card pattern.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises an outcome counter and a per-scenario trace from `show()` to `catch` — both are present and update on every run.

edge_cases.rationale: Three flavours of OperationError are differentiated, but the catch snippet references `NotSupportedError` / `SecurityError` and the page never produces one. There is also no probe of the legacy collapse-to-AbortError behaviour the feature is supposed to fix.

references.rationale: The see-also section links to sibling concepts only — the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 link is absent, which violates the self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from variables and `color-mix` against tokens. But the verdict banners use literal `✓ ↩ ⚠` glyphs as state icons; the convention is inline SVG with `currentColor`.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also lists only siblings — no chromestatus anchor on the page. That breaks the slug-recovery script that walks each `index.html` to find the ID. Add a `ChromeStatus entry` list item alongside the existing siblings.

openQuestions.title: Replace glyph icons with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: Verdict banners use `✓`, `↩`, `⚠` characters in their text. Convention is inline SVG so colour, accessibility, and dark-mode all work cleanly. Replace each glyph with a small SVG inside the verdict-banner block.

openQuestions.title: Add a `pre-Chrome-149 mode` toggle
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of this feature is that previously every failure surfaced as AbortError. A toggle that re-runs the same scenarios but coerces all OperationError outcomes back to AbortError would make the value of the change visible to a visitor who didn't already know the history.

openQuestions.title: Exercise NotSupportedError / SecurityError fall-through
openQuestions.detail: The merchant catch snippet explicitly branches on those names in its `else` block, but no scenario ever triggers one. Add a `payment method not supported` scenario (use a method URL not registered) so the third branch is observed, not just documented.

openQuestions.title: Surface the parsed err.message JSON
openQuestions.detail: OperationError variants encode their detail in a structured `err.message`. Add a `raw err.message` row to each verdict so the visitor sees the JSON envelope the handler emits, then the parsed object the merchant page consumes.

summary: Tight, well-structured replay UI with a real handler and a useful counter, but it skips the chromestatus reference link and leans on emoji-style glyphs that the design rules forbid. A pre-149 simulation toggle would also amplify the spec story.
